source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Please choose the SMILES expression of the transition-state structure shown in the image, ignoring the arrows. <image_1>
extraction_notes: answer field is an option letter and was mapped to the corresponding option text
image_1 contains a PIL image object representation, not a file path or URL

issues: metadata.extraction_notes indicates image_1 was a PIL image object representation, not a file path or URL.

image_dependency: Question explicitly asks for the SMILES of the structure "shown in the image".
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual content is primary.
The task depends on reading a chemical diagram/transition-state depiction rather than extracting answerable text alone.
multi_step: Solver must interpret the depicted chemical structure and then match it against 4 SMILES options.
Choices differ on chemically meaningful details such as charge/protonation, bonding notation, and stereochemical slash notation, so comparison is not purely lexical.
Question says to ignore arrows, implying at least one filtering step before structure-to-choice matching.
verifiability: This is effectively a 4-choice classification task with a single provided answer string that exactly matches one option.
answer_type is short_text and answer_present=true, so the final output format is constrained and easy to check.
registry_passed=true and the asset record confirms one existing image, supporting downstream validation setup.

risk_flags: Asset registry issues note that metadata.extraction_notes references a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL.
Image is relatively small (270x246), which may make fine-grained chemical notation harder to inspect.

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and a primary image are all present, so the sample retains core semantic support and should not be rejected.
The task is highly image-dependent, and the available image is relatively small (270x246), which raises recoverability risk for fine-grained chemical notation.
Asset registry issues around empty metadata image paths and the PIL-object extraction note indicate provenance/asset-quality concerns, so it is safer to defer than to prioritize highly.
Verifiability is strong because the answer is present as short text and appears easy to check against the prompt/options.
